questions.question: Why is a 70B model's decoding only ~35 tokens/sec on an H100 despite idle compute units?
options: Memory bandwidth is the ceiling: every token loads 70B weights from HBM for one step of arithmetic producing one token — speculative decoding converts this latency problem into a solvable throughput problem
The KV cache is too small
questions.explanation: One verifier forward pass over prefix + N drafts produces up to N+1 accepted tokens — amortizing the weight-loading cost that dominates decode.

questions.question: What does the Leviathan theorem guarantee, even with a terrible draft?
options: Accept d ~ p with probability min(1, q(d)/p(d)), on reject sample from the normalized residual (q−p)₊ — the output is distributed EXACTLY according to the verifier q regardless of draft quality; a worse draft just rejects more often
questions.explanation: Identical, not approximate — the entire reason production stacks can ship it as a pure latency optimization with zero quality risk.

questions.question: What is the single biggest speedup lever?
options: Acceptance rate α: at fixed N=5, going from α=0.6 (vanilla draft) to α=0.9 (EAGLE-3) takes expected accepted tokens from 2.2 to 4.1 per verifier forward — nearly 2x more throughput from the same verifier
questions.explanation: E[accepted] = (1−α^(N+1))/(1−α); wall time per token is (N·c+1)/E[accepted]. For α=0.8, c=0.05, optimal N≈5-7 at 3.2x; α=0.95 pushes 5x.

questions.question: What limitation did each EAGLE generation remove?
options: EAGLE-1: draft reads the verifier's own hidden states instead of being an independent small LLM (α 0.6→0.7-0.8); EAGLE-2: dynamic draft TREES scored via tree attention with adaptive length (α>0.85); EAGLE-3: drops feature-matching loss for direct token prediction + training-time test feeding the draft its own predictions
questions.explanation: EAGLE-3's TTT aligns train and inference distributions to stop error accumulation — measured up to 6.5x on chat and +38% throughput at batch 64 in SGLang.

questions.question: What must a correct speculative implementation handle besides accept/reject?
options: Rolling the KV cache BACK on rejection (the verifier already wrote states for discarded draft positions) and emitting the bonus token from q_{N+1} on full acceptance
questions.explanation: The cache rollback is the classic implementation bug: forget it, and rejected draft tokens poison subsequent attention.
